# Copilot code review — devantler-tech homebrew-tap

This repo is the **Homebrew tap** for devantler-tech tools (`brew tap devantler-tech/tap`). Despite the
name it distributes **Casks**, not formulas: the `ksail` CLI and the `ksail-desktop` app. Canonical
conventions live in [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md); this is the concise review checklist — flag violations
of the rules below.

## Always (every PR)

- **Conventional-Commit PR title** (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`/`ci:`/`chore:`/`refactor:`/`test:`). The
  squash-merge title feeds the changelog, so a non-conventional or bracket-prefixed title is a defect.
- **Root-cause fixes only.** Flag anything that masks a failure: checks skipped or set
  `continue-on-error` to dodge red CI, `--no-verify`, silenced audits.
- **No secrets in plaintext** — flag committed tokens, keys, or credentials.
- PRs from the Daily AI Assistant carry a `> 🤖 Generated by the Daily AI Assistant` line — expected,
  not a finding.

## Casks are GoReleaser-generated — `# DO NOT EDIT`

- `Casks/ksail.rb` and `Casks/ksail-desktop.rb` are **auto-generated by GoReleaser** and carry a
  `# DO NOT EDIT` header. The upstream GoReleaser cask template (in the tool's repo) is the source of
  truth for their content.
- **Never hand-edit `version`/`url`/`sha256` to chase a release.** Releases open their **own** automated
  Cask-bump PR; a manual bump races that automation and risks a wrong `sha256`. Flag any human PR that
  edits version/url/sha by hand.
- **Don't hand-close superseded `goreleaser/<cask>-v*` bump PRs.** `close-superseded-cask-bumps.yaml`
  closes the ones at-or-below `main`'s current version automatically; flag a PR that manually closes them.
- A genuine Cask-content fix (deprecated DSL, stanza shape) must change the **upstream GoReleaser
  template**, not just this generated file — a here-only edit will be overwritten on next release. Flag
  generated-file edits that aren't mirrored upstream.
- Each Cask should keep its shape: `version`, per-platform `sha256` + `url` → the matching
  `devantler-tech/ksail` release asset, a skipped `livecheck` (auto-generated on release), the
  `binary`/`app` stanza, and the `postflight` that strips the macOS quarantine xattr.

## Validation / CI

- CI runs **`brew audit --strict --online`** on every Cask (macOS) and aggregates it into the required
  checks gate — a PR that breaks audit must not merge. Flag changes that would fail audit (bad `url`,
  `homepage`, `desc`, license, unreachable asset).
- CI does **not** yet gate `brew style`: the GoReleaser-generated Casks trip ~20 RuboCop offenses
  fixable only in the upstream template, so a style gate would block every PR until that lands. Don't
  ask for `brew style` fixes on the generated Casks here.
- Keep `README.md`'s Cask/Description table in sync with the Casks present — flag drift.

## GitHub Actions / CI

- Pin third-party actions to a SHA; keep workflows `actionlint`-clean and `permissions:` least-privilege.
  Bundle dependency bumps and call out majors.

## Agent & instruction files (`AGENTS.md`, this file)

- Keep them internally consistent: a changed path, command, label, or convention in `AGENTS.md` must
  match this file — flag drift in either direction.
- Copilot code review reads `.github/copilot-instructions.md` at **≤4000 chars** and does **not** read
  `AGENTS.md` — keep this file concise and standalone; flag a PR that bloats it past the limit.

This file is self-contained for Copilot review. When a rule here is ambiguous, flag it and request
human clarification; humans can consult [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md) and its `## Maintenance` section.
